About — The Firm

Brookings, if Brookings were an operator.

Power In Numbers is a research and decision-engineering firm. It does not write recommendations and let the principal carry them forward. It writes recommendations, names the counterfactual it rejects, and commits to the audit standard by which the recommendation can be re-tested two years later.

Founded 2001 · Operating record continuous · Discipline named publicly 2003

§ 01 What the firm is

Three engines, one standard.

The firm operates three engines, each calibrated to a distinct principal type. The Market Entry Intelligence Decision Protocol is built for an individual decision-maker — a clinician, founder, or operator evaluating a high-consequence move. VERIDEX BI USA is the firm-grade domestic engine, calibrated to US-domiciled enterprises and operating ventures. The Sovereign Intelligence Decision Protocol is the sovereign and multilateral engine, calibrated to fiscal frameworks, public-finance instruments, and macro-structural evaluations.

All three engines clear the same canonical 22-gate VERIDEX validation standard. The engines differ in scope, instruments, and comparator sets — not in the audit standard their output must satisfy.

What the firm sells.

The firm sells decisions. To be more precise: the firm sells the audit trail that lets a principal make a decision, defend it, and refer back to it later when the world has changed.

A Power In Numbers deliverable, in any of its three engine configurations, comprises four constants — a development question named without ambiguity, a methodology applied with the framework and gates named, an output paired with the counterfactual it rejects, and a separated Decision-Maker Briefing in its own artefact. Where the four constants are present, the work is a Power In Numbers deliverable. Where they are not, it is not.

§ 02 The discipline, on the record

01

Primary-source standard.

Statistics trace to source. Institutions are named. Temporal provenance is tagged. The same five-source rule that governs the firm's deliverables governs its public writing.

02

Counterfactual on the record.

Every recommendation is paired with the position it rejects, in the same paragraph. A reader can audit not only what the firm chose, but what it set down.

03

A separated reader-action line.

Where the work recommends an action, that action lives in its own block — the Decision-Maker Briefing the firm requires by VERIDEX Gate 22 — never as a formatting convention.

04

One principal, one engagement.

Every engagement names a single decision-maker on the receiving side. The deliverable is written for that reader. The firm does not run multi-stakeholder mandates without a named principal.

§ 03 What the firm commits to publicly

Method, record, ventures, library.

The firm commits to four pieces of public infrastructure. None are gated. Each can be referenced, audited, or critiqued by a reader who arrives without an introduction.

A method made legible.

CROWD POWERED — the firm's twelve-step operating framework, named publicly in 2003 — is documented openly at /methodology/framework. The 22-gate VERIDEX validation standard is enumerated in full at /methodology/validation. The eight-phase curriculum that the firm teaches is published at /methodology/curriculum. None of these are paywalled.

A track record that reads as evidence.

The firm's history is documented as a six-pattern timeline at /track-record, beginning with a 2001 employee-advocacy programme and reaching 2026 with the Market Entry Intelligence Decision Protocol Stage-3 report at the Nexten Summit Accra. Each entry pairs the firm's earlier implementation with the mainstream emergence that followed, and where possible, with a primary-source artefact.

Firm-built ventures held to the same standard.

The firm operates three of its own ventures — iMused (music attribution), Equity Guardians (homeowner advocacy), and the 144K Collective (philanthropic capital aggregation). Each is written up at /case-studies with the same four anchors as a sovereign engagement.

A library, not a content archive.

The library at /library is a publishing engine, not a marketing function. Every entry is held to the firm's editorial standard: a development question, primary-source citation, a counterfactual considered, and a separated reader-action line.

§ 04 Origin, briefly

The firm was founded by Jay Davis in 2001, around an employee-advocacy programme that — in retrospect — operated as the firm\'s first stakeholder-capitalism implementation. The discipline was named publicly as CROWD POWERED in 2003. The validation standard was hardened into VERIDEX over the decade that followed. The firm\'s curriculum — eight phases, taught to the same standard the firm produces against — has been delivered continuously since.

The firm\'s public throughline runs through Dr. Janelle Thompson, profiled in the firm\'s Go Local Magazine Edition 1 in February 2011 as the CEO of Epiphany Wellness, returning in 2026 as the principal of the Market Entry Protocol Stage-3 reference deliverable presented at the Nexten Summit Accra. A fifteen-year primary-source relationship — from a 2011 magazine profile to a 2026 audit-grade decision artefact — is the structural argument the firm cares about. The methodology is not a deck the firm rents to a client for an engagement window. It is a discipline the firm practices on the same people, over time, with the same standard.

Selected dates

  • 2001 Firm founded; employee-advocacy programme.
  • 2003 CROWD POWERED named publicly.
  • 2008 VerSes — proto social-token implementation.
  • 2010 Go Local Magazine — flagship issue.
  • 2011 Dr. Thompson, Edition 1 profile.
  • 2023 Equity Guardians established.
  • 2025 144K Collective; iMused platform launch.
  • 2026 Market Entry Protocol Stage-3 · Nexten Summit Accra.
